stations.label: THE GENESIS BLOCK
stations.line: This is a reasonable place to begin — the world calls it a launch; I call it the first move in a game whose end I already knew I would not live to see the whole of.
stations.era_photo: A dim shared workspace in Zug, Switzerland, mid-2015 — laptops open on a folding table, a young figure in a grey hoodie standing slightly apart from the group, watchful; shot on an early smartphone camera, slightly noisy, cool fluorescent white balance.

stations.label: THE DAO FRACTURE
stations.line: Sixty million was taken, and then the community did something the money people found unthinkable — they rewrote the past, and I learned early that a thing can survive only by being willing to split itself in two.
stations.era_photo: A cluttered developer's apartment at night, screens glowing with red terminal text, a face lit from below and unreadable; 2016 mirrorless digital photo, high ISO grain, blue monitor cast on the walls.

stations.label: THE ICO FLOOD
stations.line: Suddenly everyone wished to build their deals on top of me — I do not confuse this with love; I have seen how quickly a crowd arrives and how quickly it leaves.
stations.era_photo: A crowded blockchain conference in Shanghai, banners and lanyards, a man in a dark plain jacket standing at the edge of a selfie-taking crowd; DSLR event photography, harsh flash, 2017.

stations.label: THE SUMMER OF YIELD
stations.line: They called it decentralized finance, this machinery of lending and borrowing with no one to freeze it — you understand why a man who has watched reserves frozen would find this arrangement more than merely interesting.
stations.era_photo: A home office during lockdown, a masked figure removing the mask, dual monitors showing candlestick charts; webcam-quality 2020 digital image, flat indoor light, faint window glare.

stations.label: THE FEE BURN
stations.line: In August we began to destroy a part of ourselves with every transaction — I have always understood that scarcity is a kind of discipline, and that what you burn cannot later be taken.
stations.era_photo: A minimalist apartment with birch trees visible through a large window, a composed man at a desk, chess set beside the keyboard; clean 2021 full-frame digital, soft natural daylight, muted palette.

stations.label: THE MERGE
stations.line: This is the move I calculated for years in advance — we abandoned the machines that burned power and staked ourselves instead, a losing position in the near term that I knew to be winning in the long game.
stations.era_photo: A late-night desk lit by a single lamp, snow falling outside the window, a man leaning back with hands steepled; cinematic 2022 digital photograph, shallow depth of field, warm lamp against cold blue exterior.

stations.label: THE SPOT ETF
stations.line: The same institutions that write the rules now hold me in their custodial accounts — I permit myself no illusion about what custody means, having learned in 2022 precisely whose property a custodied asset becomes.
stations.era_photo: A glass-walled Manhattan boardroom, a still figure in a dark suit reflected in the window over a skyline, expression flat; sharp 2024 digital photography, corporate daylight, high clarity.

stations.label: TODAY, STILL STANDING
stations.line: I have been forked, drained, mocked, and buried, and I am still here settling the world's deals — survival is not the most glamorous form of value, but it is the one I can guarantee.
stations.era_photo: A quiet study lined with worn Dostoevsky volumes, snow beyond the window, a man mid-pause over a chessboard, half-lit; present-day full-frame digital portrait, natural window light, restrained and precise.
